signals.reasoning: This is a failed live trade. BLUR has gone from a peak gain of about $1.21 to a current loss near $-0.17, retaining none of peak P&L, and the request flags it urgent. With the event veto now back on, there is even less reason to keep hoping for recovery. Close it.
BTC is stable and liquid, but the board still marks it no-trade and the live veto argues against opening new risk. There is no strong directional edge here.
ETH is mildly constructive but still low-energy, with positive funding and no-trade status on the board. Not compelling enough to act on during a veto window.
SOL has slightly better funding support than ETH, but the board still says no-trade and the directional edge remains weak. Easy hold.

ZEC is one of the more interesting bullish names on the sheet with strong momentum and negative funding, but the live veto is exactly when extended continuation trades should be downgraded. Watchlist, not entry.
MERL is moving, but liquidity is thin, spread is wide, and the board is still neutral. Not worth touching here.
ZEN has decent strength, but funding is positive and the board remains neutral. Under veto conditions it stays watch-only.
DASH has good volume and momentum, but the setup is still neutral rather than compelling. Not a priority while veto is active.
TURBO still has a real squeeze-continuation profile with negative funding and strong crowding, but the live veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta continuation exposure.
PEOPLE is too thin and too neutral to matter. No strong edge either way.
TNSR still screens as bullish mechanically, but the event veto overrides the temptation to chase it. Strong watchlist long, not a trade I would open right now.
